metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Innistrad Remastered is a "greatest hits" collection from Magic's seven Innistrad sets. The curated draft format combines mechanics and cards from across Innistrad's history, available for paper MTG and Magic Online but not MTG Arena.

color_notes.Note: INR is heavily archetype-driven; rank pairs rather than colors. BR appears strongest; RG appears weakest. Draft mana fixing (Evolving Wilds, Traveler's Amulet, Wild-Field Scarecrow) for 3-color decks.

key_gameplay_notes: Self-mill, madness, sacrifice, and token strategies have strong support.
Prioritize card quality and synergy balance — avoid forcing weak synergies.
Recognize your draft lane early and commit to it.
Value creatures like Tower Geist and efficient removal are universally strong.
Madness enablers (Faithless Looting, Olivia's Dragoon, Furyblade Vampire, Lightning Axe) feed BR.

mechanics.name: Transforming DFCs
mechanics.summary: Double-faced cards that flip when conditions are met (e.g., Delver of Secrets / Insectile Aberration). Spread across all colors and archetypes.
mechanics.performance: Individually strong; the flip side often represents free value.

key_examples.name: Delver of Secrets
key_examples.type_line: Creature — Human Wizard // Creature — Human Insect

mechanics.name: Disturb
mechanics.summary: Returns creatures as spirits or auras from the graveyard for a specific cost. Appears on 4 cards; supports WU Spirits.
mechanics.performance: Recurring value mechanic — every Disturb card is essentially two cards.

mechanics.name: Meld
mechanics.summary: Two specific cards fuse into one during combat. 3 pairs total; rare mechanic.
mechanics.performance: Build-around bomb when both halves are drafted; otherwise role-players.

mechanics.name: Emerge
mechanics.summary: Cast colorless creatures by sacrificing creatures, reducing cost. 3 commons + 3 rares.
mechanics.performance: Solid tempo play; Wretched Gryff is a top blue common.

mechanics.name: Madness
mechanics.summary: Cast from graveyard when discarded instead of going to the graveyard. Black/red cards form the core BR mechanic.
mechanics.performance: Anchors the top-tier BR Vampires/Madness archetype.

mechanics.name: Escalate
mechanics.summary: Pay more for additional effects. Appears on 4 cards, mostly red.

mechanics.name: Flashback
mechanics.summary: Recast spells from graveyard by paying flashback cost. Instants/sorceries in all colors.
mechanics.performance: Strong card advantage; pairs with self-mill in UG.

mechanics.name: Investigate
mechanics.summary: Create Clue tokens that draw cards when sacrificed. Minor theme; 4 cards.

mechanics.name: Blood Tokens
mechanics.summary: Sacrifice to discard and draw (rummage effect). Synergy with madness.

mechanics.name: Undying
mechanics.summary: Returns from death with +1/+1 counter. 2 commons: Young Wolf, Butcher Ghoul.
mechanics.performance: Solid value creatures; play extra copies of these.

mechanics.name: Miracle
mechanics.summary: Cast for reduced cost if drawn as top card of library. 2 cards; rarely impactful.

mechanics.name: Soulbond
mechanics.summary: Pair creatures together for a benefit. 2 cards: Lightning Mauler, Deadeye Navigator.
mechanics.performance: Rare interaction with high upside when drafted.

tier_1.name: BR Vampires / Madness
tier_1.strategy: Combine vampire payoffs with madness enablers. Bloodtithe Harvester is the signpost. Madness chains (Faithless Looting → Fiery Temper) snowball games quickly.

tier_2.name: WB Attrition / Aristocrats
tier_2.strategy: Grind opponents out with token generation and sacrifice synergies. Fleshtaker is the signpost. Lingering Souls, Thraben Inspector, and Butcher Ghoul form the core.

tier_2.name: UB Zombies / Good Stuff
tier_2.strategy: Midrange/control with efficient removal and zombie payoffs. Bladestitched Skaab is the signpost.

tier_2.name: UG Self-Mill
tier_2.strategy: Mill yourself and turn the graveyard into a resource. Vilespawn Spider is the signpost. Often splashes into GW or BG for additional payoffs.

tier_2.name: WU Spirits
tier_2.strategy: No signpost uncommon; cheap fliers and tempo. Lean on Disturb and Apothecary Geist for value.

tier_3.name: GW Humans / Tokens
tier_3.strategy: Join the Dance is the signpost. Limited human payoffs; rely on efficient creatures with removal.

key_cards.name: Mayor of Avabruck
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Human Advisor Werewolf // Creature — Werewolf

tier_3.name: UR Spells
tier_3.strategy: Wandering Mind is the signpost. Two builds: Prowess aggro (Delver of Secrets, Lantern Bearer, Silent Departure, Festival Crasher, Ancestral Anger) or Control (Seize the Storm, Think Twice, Syncopate, Tower Geist + removal).

key_cards.name: Delver of Secrets
key_cards.type_line: Creature — Human Wizard // Creature — Human Insect

tier_3.name: RW Aggro / Tokens
tier_3.strategy: Markov Waltzer is the signpost. Focus on efficient curve and combat tricks.

tier_4.name: BG Morbid
tier_4.strategy: Only 4 morbid cards (Tragic Slip, Festerhide Boar, Morkrut Banshee, Skirsdag High Priest). Alternative: graveyard strategy with Spider Spawning, Splinterfright.

tier_4.name: RG Werewolves
tier_4.strategy: No signpost uncommon; weaker archetype with limited payoffs. Focus on a generic stompy plan.

{R} Red.name: Conduit of Storms
{R} Red.type_line: Creature — Werewolf Horror // Creature — Eldrazi Werewolf

{W} White.name: Ambitious Farmhand
{W} White.type_line: Creature — Human Peasant // Creature — Human Knight

{G} Green.name: Duskwatch Recruiter
{G} Green.type_line: Creature — Human Warrior Werewolf // Creature — Werewolf

notable_rares.name: Archangel Avacyn
notable_rares.type_line: Legendary Creature — Angel // Legendary Creature — Angel

notable_rares.name: Arlinn Kord
notable_rares.type_line: Legendary Planeswalker — Arlinn // Legendary Planeswalker — Arlinn

general_tips: BR Vampires/Madness is the strongest archetype — pivot into it if open.
Self-mill, madness, sacrifice, and token strategies all have real support; pure goodstuff piles are weaker.
Recognize your draft lane early and commit; INR rewards coherence.
Mana fixing exists (Evolving Wilds, Traveler's Amulet, Wild-Field Scarecrow) for 3-color builds.
Avoid forcing RG Werewolves or BG Morbid without strong signals — both lanes are thin.
Tower Geist and equivalent value creatures play in every blue deck.
